esp_now_master_init.tooltip: Initialize as ESP-NOW host (broadcast sender) with one click, automatically configure WiFi and ESP-NOW

esp_now_slave_init.tooltip: Initialize as ESP-NOW slave (receiver) with one click, automatically configure WiFi, ESP-NOW and register new devices

esp_now_broadcast_message.tooltip: Broadcast messages to all devices in the network (needs to be initialized as a host first)

esp_now_on_message_received.message0: When an ESP-NOW message is received
esp_now_on_message_received.tooltip: Global receive callback, any ESP-NOW message will be triggered. Available in callback: received message content, message length, whether to broadcast, sender MAC

esp_now_received_message.tooltip: Get the received message content in the global receive callback

esp_now_received_message_len.tooltip: Get message length in global receive callback

esp_now_received_is_broadcast.tooltip: Determine whether it is a broadcast message in the global receive callback

esp_now_received_sender_mac.tooltip: Get the sender's MAC address in the global receive callback

esp_now_reply_message.tooltip: Reply to the last device that sent the message (used in or after the receive callback)

esp_now_node_init.message0: ESP-NOW initialized as bidirectional node channel %1
esp_now_node_init.tooltip: Initialized as a bidirectional node, it can both broadcast and receive messages.

esp_now_begin.tooltip: Initialize ESP-NOW protocol, using default configuration

esp_now_begin_with_pmk.tooltip: Initialize the ESP-NOW protocol and set the master key (16 bytes) for encrypted communication

esp_now_wifi_init.tooltip: Initialize WiFi mode and channel (ESP-NOW needs to configure WiFi first)

esp_now_create_peer.tooltip: Create an ESP-NOW peer device (need to specify the other party's MAC address)

esp_now_create_peer_advanced.message0:
Create peer device %1 MAC address %2
Channel %3 Encryption Key %4
esp_now_create_peer_advanced.tooltip: Create ESP-NOW peer with encryption (LMK requires 16 bytes)

esp_now_create_broadcast_peer.tooltip: Create a broadcast peer that sends messages to all devices on the network

esp_now_send.tooltip: Sends a string message to the specified peer device

esp_now_send_data.tooltip: Send raw data to the specified peer device

esp_now_on_receive.message0: When the peer device %1 receives the message
esp_now_on_receive.tooltip: Sets the callback function for the peer device to receive messages. Variables can be used in the callback: esp_now_data (message content), esp_now_len (message length), esp_now_broadcast (whether to broadcast)

esp_now_on_sent.message0: When the peer device %1 message sending is complete
esp_now_on_sent.tooltip: Set the callback function when the message is sent. Variables can be used in the callback: esp_now_success (whether the sending is successful)

esp_now_on_new_peer.message0: When a new peer message is received
esp_now_on_new_peer.tooltip: Triggered when an unknown device message is received. Variables can be used in the callback: esp_now_src_mac (sender MAC), esp_now_data (message content), esp_now_len (message length)

esp_now_received_data.tooltip: Get the received message content (string) in the receive callback

esp_now_received_len.tooltip: Get the received message length in the receive callback

esp_now_is_broadcast.tooltip: Determine whether it is a broadcast message in the receive callback

esp_now_send_success.tooltip: Determine whether the message is sent successfully in the send callback

esp_now_src_mac.tooltip: Get sender MAC address in new peer callback

esp_now_serial_create.message0: Create ESP-NOW serial port %1 Partner MAC %2 Channel %3 Working mode %4
esp_now_serial_create.tooltip: Create an ESP-NOW serial communication object, similar to how Serial is used

esp_now_serial_available.message0: ESP-NOW serial port %1 Number of readable bytes
esp_now_serial_available.tooltip: Get the number of bytes that can be read in the ESP-NOW serial port receive buffer

esp_now_serial_available_for_write.message0: ESP-NOW serial port %1 Number of writable bytes
esp_now_serial_available_for_write.tooltip: Get the remaining space of the ESP-NOW serial port sending buffer

esp_now_serial_read.message0: ESP-NOW serial port %1 reads one byte
esp_now_serial_read.tooltip: Read a byte from the ESP-NOW serial port

esp_now_serial_read_string.tooltip: Read all available data from ESP-NOW serial port as string

esp_now_serial_read_string_until.message0: ESP-NOW serial port %1 reads the string until %2
esp_now_serial_read_string_until.tooltip: Read data from the ESP-NOW serial port until the specified character is encountered

esp_now_serial_print.tooltip: Send data through ESP-NOW serial port

esp_now_serial_println.message0: ESP-NOW serial port %1 sends %2 and wraps the line
esp_now_serial_println.tooltip: Send data through ESP-NOW serial port and wrap

esp_now_serial_write.message0: ESP-NOW serial port %1 sends raw data %2
esp_now_serial_write.tooltip: Send raw data via ESP-NOW serial port

esp_now_quick_broadcast.tooltip: Quickly broadcast messages to all devices in the network (automatic initialization)

esp_now_quick_send.message0: Quickly send message to MAC %1 Content %2 Channel %3
esp_now_quick_send.tooltip: Quickly send messages to the specified MAC address device (automatic initialization)

esp_now_get_mac.tooltip: Get the WiFi MAC address of this machine

esp_now_get_max_data_len.tooltip: Get the maximum data length supported by the ESP-NOW protocol (usually 250 bytes)

esp_now_get_peer_count.tooltip: Get the number of registered peer devices

esp_now_get_version.tooltip: Get the ESP-NOW protocol version number
